title: carex muricata subsp. pairae
intro: Carex muricata subsp. pairae, also known as Carex pairae, is a subspecies of sedge, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Carex muricata subsp. pairae include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
